steps.title: Identify the key warning
steps.content: The passage cautions that small sample sizes can "contribute to reports of exaggerated effects." Pimentel's study used a sample of only 12 fish.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The question asks what the text "most directly suggests" about Pimentel's conclusion. The text links small samples to biased, exaggerated findings — apply that logic to each option.
  • Choice A — It was based on the same mean sample size used in many earlier studies of fish species.: the passage never compares Pimentel's sample to earlier studies, so this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — It likely has broad implications for other species of fish besides *Sparus aurata*.: the passage says nothing about other species — an unsupported inference, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — It may overstate the effect of ocean acidification on the behavior of *Sparus aurata*.: directly mirrors the passage's warning that small samples "contribute to reports of exaggerated effects," so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — It has been largely invalidated by results obtained from similar studies.: the passage flags potential bias, not full invalidation — this overclaims, so fails. ✗
C is correct.
